title: Lamb Rogan Josh

overview.description: A classic Kashmiri lamb curry made with tender lamb, yoghurt and aromatic spices, with a deep red colour from Kashmiri chilli rather than tomatoes.

ingredients: 1kg lamb shoulder, cut into large pieces
method: Remove the lamb from the refrigerator and allow it to come towards room temperature.
Heat the oil or ghee in a large heavy-based pan.
Add the bay leaves, black cardamom, green cardamom, cinnamon, cloves and cumin seeds.
Add the lamb in batches if necessary and brown it lightly on all sides.
Add the Kashmiri chilli powder, hot chilli powder if using, ground fennel, ground ginger and ground cumin.
Stir briefly to coat the lamb, taking care not to burn the spices.
Add the yoghurt a little at a time, stirring constantly after each addition so it does not split.
Continue cooking gently for several minutes until the yoghurt has thickened and the oil begins to separate.
Cover and cook gently for 1½–2 hours, or until the lamb is completely tender.
Stir occasionally and add a little more water if necessary.
Remove the lid towards the end of cooking if the gravy needs reducing.

learning.did-you-know: Rogan josh is associated with Kashmiri cuisine and is traditionally made in several regional styles. The deep red colour can come from mild Kashmiri chilli rather than a large amount of heat.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to build a deeply flavoured curry from whole spices, yoghurt and slow-cooked lamb while separating colour, aroma and heat as different elements of the dish.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A classic Kashmiri lamb dish that demonstrates how a curry can develop extraordinary depth without relying on tomatoes, onions or garlic.
chapman-tips: Use lamb shoulder with some fat and connective tissue; it becomes wonderfully tender during the long cooking time.
Don't rush the yoghurt addition. Adding it gradually over low heat helps prevent it splitting.
Kashmiri chilli powder gives colour and a fruity chilli flavour with relatively little heat.
The optional hot chilli powder allows heat to be increased separately from the colour.
Ground fennel is an important part of the characteristic Kashmiri flavour.
The finished gravy should be rich and aromatic rather than thick like a tomato-based restaurant curry.
Rogan josh often tastes even better after resting or reheating the following day.
This is a good dish for teaching that colour and heat are not the same thing.
notes.batch-cooking: Freezes well. Cool completely before freezing in airtight portions. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator and reheat gently until piping hot.
notes.leftovers: Reheat gently and serve with fresh rice or bread. The lamb can also be shredded and used as a filling for a roti or paratha.
